evals.prompt:
Write a description for a new run called Hyde Park Road Loop. Here are my notes:
- Starts at the Hyde Park trailhead at the bottom of the mountain (lower elevation, accessible earlier in season)
- Climbs steeply up the paved Hyde Park Road for about 2 miles, then hits the trail near the ski resort
- Really runnable road section but your quads will be fried on the descent
- The return is on singletrack through beautiful ponderosa forest, lots of shade
- Very popular with both hikers and runners on weekends
- Great option when higher trails are snowed in
- About 6 miles, 1400ft gain, difficulty: blue
evals.expected_output: A 2-4 sentence description in the site voice: conversational, first-person opinion allowed, addresses reader as 'you', mentions body sensations (burning quads), reward after suffering, practical tip about season accessibility. No headers or bullet points.

assertions.name: uses_second_person
assertions.description: Description uses 'you' to address the reader directly at least once

assertions.name: mentions_physical_sensation
assertions.description: Description includes at least one physical body sensation (burning, gasping, aching, etc.)

assertions.name: no_generic_opener
assertions.description: Description does not open with a generic phrase like 'This trail offers' or 'Located in' or 'Nestled among'

assertions.name: correct_length
assertions.description: Description is between 2 and 5 sentences, not a wall of text

assertions.name: no_bullet_points
assertions.description: Output contains no bullet points, dashes, headers, or markdown formatting

evals.prompt:
I need a description for the Atalaya Mountain run. Key points:
- Classic Santa Fe hike that most locals have done
- Steep rocky climb to the top of Atalaya, 9121ft summit
- Views of the city and the Jemez Mountains to the west
- About 6 miles out and back, 1900ft gain
- Trailhead is right in the city, behind St. John's College
- Can be extremely hot in summer — best in spring and fall
- Trail can be crowded on weekends especially in good weather
- Difficulty: black
evals.expected_output: A 2-4 sentence description that captures the accessible-but-serious nature of Atalaya, the city views as reward, heat warning, and crowding. Voice should match site: direct, specific, with an insider feel.

assertions.name: mentions_views_or_reward
assertions.description: Description mentions the views or some form of reward/payoff for the climb

assertions.name: includes_practical_warning
assertions.description: Description includes a practical tip or warning (heat, crowds, season, etc.)

assertions.name: no_generic_opener
assertions.description: Description does not open with a generic phrase like 'This trail offers' or 'Located in' or 'Nestled among'

assertions.name: no_bullet_points
assertions.description: Output contains no bullet points, dashes, headers, or markdown formatting

evals.prompt:
Write a run description for a gnarly one I call the Tesuque Death March. Notes:
- Epic point-to-point that requires a car shuttle
- Starts at Aspen Basin (10,300ft), summits Tesuque Peak (12,040ft) via the radio towers
- Then descends all the way down into Tesuque village (7,200ft)
- Total descent is 4,800ft over about 9 miles — absolutely destroys your quads
- The upper section near the radio towers is exposed and technical
- The lower canyon section through Tesuque Creek is magical, cool shade, wildflowers in July
- Extremely long season for the lower portion but upper section is snow-covered until June
- Difficulty: double-black
evals.expected_output: 2-5 sentences that convey the epic nature of the descent, the contrast between brutal upper section and beautiful lower canyon, the shuttle logistics, the quad destruction, and perhaps the reward of the creek section. Should have humor and personality.

assertions.name: conveys_epic_scale
assertions.description: Description conveys the scale/difficulty of the run, not just 'it's hard' but something specific about what makes it hard

assertions.name: mentions_reward_or_beautiful_section
assertions.description: Description mentions the beautiful lower section, creek, wildflowers, or some form of payoff

assertions.name: no_bullet_points
assertions.description: Output contains no bullet points, dashes, headers, or markdown formatting

assertions.name: no_generic_opener
assertions.description: Description does not open with a generic phrase like 'This trail offers' or 'Located in' or 'Nestled among'
